site_analysis_review.platform_notes: Custom ASP.NET platform confirmed by .aspx URLs, inline phe.config JavaScript object, and server-rendered HTML. Site analysis correctly identified the platform.
site_analysis_review.anti_bot_notes: No anti-bot protection detected. Direct HTTP returns full page content. Only OneTrust for cookie consent (compliance, not anti-bot). Confirmed by both probe and browser navigation.
site_analysis_review.mechanism_notes: http_requests is the correct mechanism. Page is fully server-rendered. JSON-LD provides complete product data. No JavaScript rendering needed for any core field.

connectivity.notes: Direct HTTP works perfectly. Page is server-rendered ASP.NET with all product data in HTML source (JSON-LD, OG meta tags, inline serverSideEvents JavaScript, DOM). No proxy or browser needed.

extraction_methods.structured_data_notes: JSON-LD Product block is very rich with ALL core fields. Uses non-standard capitalized property names (Name, Mpn, Price, Description, Brand, Image, Offers) instead of standard schema.org lowercase. Code MUST use capitalized keys.

src_url.notes: Set by scraper from input_urls.json. Not extractable from page.

status_code.notes: Set by scraper from HTTP response.

scraped_at.notes: Set by scraper as ISO-8601 timestamp.

remarks.notes: Set by scraper for any warnings or notes.

title.notes: JSON-LD uses 'Name' (capital N), not standard 'name'. h1.item_title is the DOM source. OG title includes site suffix '- Women's Lingerie | Adam & Eve' that should be stripped.

price.notes: Regular/list price is in .ae-normal-price .ae-price--normal (visible when not on sale). When on sale, .ae-sale-price becomes visible and .ae-normal-price hides. There is also a .jcpoffer-pricerange showing a promotional coupon price ('Only $27.49') — this should NOT be extracted as the product price. JSON-LD Offers.Price gives the base price reliably.

original_price.notes: Only available when product is on sale. The .ae-sale-price div is hidden (class 'hide') when there's no sale. The ae-price--was span inside shows the original/regular price. When .ae-sale-price is visible, ae-normal-price is hidden. Extract ONLY if .ae-sale-price does NOT have class 'hide'. This product is NOT on sale, so original_price equals price.

currency.notes: Available in both JSON-LD and OG meta tags.

availability.notes: JSON-LD uses full URL 'http://schema.org/InStock'. OG tag uses short form 'instock'. serverSideEvents.dimension11 has human-readable 'In Stock'.

brand.notes: Brand is available from JSON-LD (nested object Product.Brand.Name), OG meta tag, and serverSideEvents. All three sources agree.

category.notes: Multiple sources available. BreadcrumbList JSON-LD has individual breadcrumb items. serverSideEvents has the full category path as a string.

breadcrumbs.notes: BreadcrumbList JSON-LD provides name and @id for each level. CSS fallback is nav.breadcrumb a elements.

examples: While scandalously exposed breasts do what they're best at, you'll cast a mesmerizing spell in hypnotic swirl patterned mesh! This babydoll's soft & stretchy, with satin bow detail and matching crotchless thong. Nylon & spandex. Imported.
description.notes: JSON-LD has the full product description. DOM has it split across multiple accordion sections. JSON-LD is the cleanest source. JSON-LD uses 'Description' (capital D).

sku.notes: JSON-LD uses 'Mpn' (capital M) for the SKU/MPN field. serverSideEvents has Sku field. No visible SKU in DOM text.

images.notes: Multiple product images in a glider/carousel slider (.ae-product-carousel .glider .glider-slide img). JSON-LD has a single main image (250x250). OG image is the main product image. Main carousel image selector: .ae-product-carousel .glider-slide.active img or first glider-slide img.

image_url.notes: Single primary image. JSON-LD provides 250x250 version. OG image is the full-size version.

rating.notes: JSON-LD has AggregateRating with RatingValue (4.2) and RatingCount (637). DOM text shows '4.25 stars out of 5'. serverSideEvents.dimension13 has 4.25. JSON-LD truncates to 4.2. The DOM/serverSideEvents value (4.25) is more precise.

review_count.notes: Available from JSON-LD (637) and serverSideEvents (637). DOM shows '(637)' in the rating section.

color_variants.notes: Colors array is in serverSideEvents JavaScript. The variant select dropdown only shows currently available combinations (7 options for this product, mixing color+size). serverSideEvents.Colors has all possible colors.

size_variants.css_fallback: select.product-variant-select options (parse size from text like 'Small/Med', 'Queen', 'Large/Xlrg')
size_variants.notes: serverSideEvents.Sizes is empty for this product. Sizes are embedded within variant option text (e.g., 'Red/Small/Med ($49.99)'). Parse from select.product-variant-select options text.

variants.js_extraction: Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('select.product-variant-select option')).filter(o => o.value).map(o => ({ value: o.value, text: o.textContent.trim(), price: o.textContent.match(/\$[\d.]+/)?.[0] }))
variants.notes: Variant select dropdown (select.product-variant-select) with options containing color/size combo and price. First option is always 'Please make a selection' with empty value. Each variant option has a numeric value (internal variant ID) and text like 'Red/Small/Med ($49.99)'.

location.notes: Not extractable from page. No warehouse/store location information on product page.

jsonld_extraction.product_jsonld_notes: Very rich Product schema with non-standard capitalized property names. Fields: Name, Mpn, Image, Description, Brand (nested object with Name), AggregateRating (RatingValue, RatingCount, BestRating, WorstRating), Offers (Price, PriceCurrency, Availability, ItemCondition, Url, ShippingDetails), Review (array of reviews). Second JSON-LD block has parse issue due to concatenated JSON after the main object — extract by finding first { to last } brace pair.

variants.variant_data_source: DOM select element with inline options
variants.variant_id_attribute: option value attribute (numeric internal ID like '290339')
variants.note: Variants are combined color+size in a single dropdown. serverSideEvents provides all possible colors separately. Size must be parsed from option text. Variant prices may differ per variant (though same for this product).

server_side_events.access_method: Parse from inline JavaScript: var serverSideEvents = [{...}]
server_side_events.notes: serverSideEvents is an inline JavaScript array pushed to dataLayer. Can be extracted by regex-parsing the script tag or evaluating in a JS context.

promo_price_warning.explanation: This is a promotional price shown with a coupon code (e.g., 'DOUBLE45'). It is NOT the actual product price. The regular/sale price structure is in .ae-normal-price and .ae-sale-price. Do NOT extract .jcpoffer-pricerange as the product price.
